factors.summary: The company is exposed to regulatory changes related to AI, which could lead to increased compliance costs, legal liability, and operational constraints.

evidence.text: The use or offering of AI technologies may result in new or expanded risks and liabilities, including enhanced government or regulatory scrutiny, litigation, compliance issues, ethical concerns, confidentiality, reputational harm and security risks. It is not possible to predict all of the risks related to the use of AI and changes in laws, rules, directives, and regulations governing the use of AI may adversely affect our ability to develop and use AI or subject us to legal liability.

factors.summary: The company faces potential litigation and regulatory risks due to the use of AI technologies.

evidence.text: The use or offering of AI technologies may result in new or expanded risks and liabilities, including enhanced government or regulatory scrutiny, litigation, compliance issues, ethical concerns, confidentiality, reputational harm and security risks.

factors.summary: The company has not provided specific forward guidance but mentions updates to risk factors.

evidence.text: Except for the updates to this risk factors set forth below, there have not been material changes in our risk factors set forth in the company’s 2022 Annual Report on Form 10-K.

factors.summary: The company is restructuring its workforce.

evidence.text: Average employee headcount decreased by 10.1 percent year-over-year.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intensifying due to excess capacity and weak demand.

evidence.text: The declines in pricing and purchased transportation costs were driven by the excess carrier capacity and continued weak consumer demand discussed in the market trends and business trends sections above.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Interest rate increases are creating headwinds through higher debt service costs.

evidence.text: Interest expense increased $15.3 million driven by a higher variable interest rates compared to the prior year. The prior year included an $11.8 million unfavorable impact of foreign currency revaluation and realized foreign currency gains and losses due to the strengthening of the U.S. Dollar versus the Euro and Yuan.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating unfavorable impacts.

evidence.text: Interest and other income/expense, net primarily consisted of interest expense of $46.8 million and a $6.0 million unfavorable impact of foreign currency revaluation and realized foreign currency gains and losses in the six months ended June 30, 2023, primarily due to foreign currency impacts on intercompany assets and liabilities.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks could create material impacts, though specifics are not detailed.

evidence.text: The company is facing challenges due to geopolitical factors, though specific details are not provided in the text.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are relatively stable, focused on software and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures consisted primarily of investments in software, which are intended to develop and deliver scalable solutions by transforming our processes, accelerate the pace of development and prioritizing data integrity, improve our customer and carrier experience, and increase efficiency to help expand our adjusted operating margins and grow the business.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable with manageable maturities.

evidence.text: We have historically generated substantial cash from operations, which has enabled us to fund our organic growth while paying cash dividends and repurchasing stock. In addition, we maintain the following debt facilities as described in Note 4, Financing Arrangements (in thousands).

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being managed conservatively.

evidence.text: The number of shares we repurchase, if any, during future periods will vary based on our cash position, other potential uses of our cash, and market conditions. Our Senior Notes, Series A and Receivables Securitization Facility have maturity dates remaining in 2023. To the extent we reduce our outstanding debt on these facilities or our other debt facilities, it may reduce the number of shares we repurchase in 2023.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving but faces challenges.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities increased in the six months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022 due to a decrease in net operating working capital driven by declining freight rates, most notably in our ocean and truckload services in addition to lower volumes in nearly all service lines as discussed in the market and business trends sections. This impact was partially offset by a decline in net income in the six months ended June 30, 2023.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are being pursued cautiously, focusing on strategic fit and growth opportunities.

evidence.text: We are continually looking for acquisitions, but those acquisitions must fit our culture and enhance our growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges from economic conditions, particularly due to weak consumer demand and excess carrier capacity, which have negatively impacted pricing and transportation costs.

evidence.text: The company does not explicitly address the impact of economic conditions, but mentions that additional risks may affect its business.

The declines in pricing and purchased transportation costs were driven by the excess carrier capacity and continued weak consumer demand discussed in the market trends and business trends sections above.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company is expanding its use of AI, which could support future growth, but organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower pricing and declining volumes across key service lines.

evidence.text: We use, and may continue to expand our use of, machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to deliver our services and operate our business.
Total revenues decreased 35.0 percent to $4.4 billion, driven primarily by lower ocean and truckload pricing.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues and direct costs decreased driven by lower pricing and purchased transportation costs in nearly all of our service lines, most notably in ocean and truckload services. In addition, volumes declined in nearly all transportation services compared to the strong results in the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company identifies the use of AI as a key sensitivity, noting risks related to integration, competition, and regulatory changes.

evidence.text: We use, and may continue to expand our use of, machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to deliver our services and operate our business. If we fail to successfully integrate AI into our platform and business processes, or if we fail to keep pace with rapidly evolving AI technological developments, including attracting and retaining talented AI developers and programmers, we may face a competitive disadvantage.

Risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, including uncertain consumer demand; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services.

The effective income tax rate for the six months ended June 30, 2023 was lower than the statutory federal income tax rate primarily due to U.S. tax credits and incentives, foreign tax credits, and the tax impact of share-based payment awards.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company is investing in AI, which could lead to increased operating expenses. However, cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, particularly in personnel expenses, which decreased by 15.2 percent to $377.3 million due to cost optimization efforts and lower variable compensation. Despite these savings, there are potential increases in operating expenses due to compliance costs with AI-related laws and regulations.

evidence.text: The cost of complying with laws and regulations governing AI could be significant and would increase our operating expenses, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition and results of operations.
Personnel expenses decreased 15.2 percent to $377.3 million, primarily due to cost optimization efforts and lower variable compensation.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to cost optimization efforts including lower average employee headcount in addition to lower variable compensation reflecting the decline in results relative to the prior year. Other SG&A expenses increased due to a $23.5 million gain from a sale-leaseback of a facility in Kansas City in the prior year and increased depreciation and amortization, travel and warehouse expenses in the current year, which was partially offset by decreased contracted services, including temporary labor.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Management has shown mixed confidence, with a new CEO appointment indicating some confidence in leadership changes, but cautiousness due to market conditions.

evidence.text: We concluded our search for a new Chief Executive Officer with the appointment of David Bozeman as our President and Chief Executive Officer and a director, effective June 26, 2023.
The effective tax rate in the quarter was 14.9 percent compared to 21.3 percent in the second quarter last year.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: The company is prioritizing investments to grow the business but requires some working capital and a relatively small amount of capital expenditures to grow. We are continually looking for acquisitions, but those acquisitions must fit our culture and enhance our growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks, including challenges integrating AI, weak consumer demand, excess capacity, and geopolitical factors. These risks could impact its competitive position and operational performance.

evidence.text: If we fail to successfully integrate AI into our platform and business processes, or if we fail to keep pace with rapidly evolving AI technological developments, including attracting and retaining talented AI developers and programmers, we may face a competitive disadvantage.

Risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, including uncertain consumer demand; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services.
The global forwarding market is facing continued weak demand and excess carrier capacity. Geopolitical factors could create material risks or opportunities.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction focused on growth and operational efficiency, with a commitment to enhancing shareholder value through quarterly dividends and share repurchases, while also acknowledging the potential challenges and opportunities associated with expanding the use of AI technologies.

evidence.text: We use, and may continue to expand our use of, machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to deliver our services and operate our business.

We are committed to our quarterly dividend and share repurchases to enhance shareholder value. Such repurchases, if any, will depend on prevailing market conditions, our liquidity requirements, contractual restrictions, and other factors.

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, with evidence of declining freight volumes and transportation services. Volumes have decreased across nearly all transportation services compared to the prior year, driven by excess carrier capacity and weak consumer demand.

evidence.text: Industry freight volumes decreased in the second quarter of 2023 compared to the second quarter of 2022.

In addition, volumes declined in nearly all transportation services compared to the strong results in the prior year. The declines in pricing and purchased transportation costs were driven by the excess carrier capacity and continued weak consumer demand.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company is facing significant challenges in maintaining its pricing power, as evidenced by declining transportation rates and reduced ability to raise prices in various service lines.

evidence.text: Significant declines in transportation rates versus the elevated levels experienced in the prior year.

Total transportation revenues and direct costs decreased driven by lower pricing and purchased transportation costs in nearly all of our service lines, most notably in ocean and truckload services. The lower adjusted gross profit per transaction was driven by the excess capacity and weak demand in the surface transportation and global forwarding markets.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across the company's transportation services, with a notable decrease in NAST truckload and LTL volume.

evidence.text: Our combined NAST truckload and less than truckload (LTL) volume decreased 2.5 percent during the second quarter of 2023.
Volumes declined in nearly all transportation services compared to the strong results in the prior year. The combined NAST truckload and LTL volume decreased 2.5 percent.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, as evidenced by declining pricing and increased costs in ocean and truckload services. Additionally, lower adjusted gross profit per transaction in truckload and ocean services, along with decreased volumes across nearly all service lines, further indicates a shift towards lower-margin segments.

evidence.text: The decline in pricing and purchased transportation costs in ocean and truckload services.

The transportation adjusted gross profits decreased due to lower adjusted gross profit per transaction in truckload and ocean services, and to a lesser extent air and LTL services, in addition to decreased volumes in nearly all service lines. The lower adjusted gross profit per transaction was driven by the excess capacity and weak demand in the surface transportation and global forwarding markets.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The transportation and global forwarding markets are experiencing negative momentum, with slowing demand and excess capacity. This is supported by elevated transportation rates and moderating consumer demand observed in the second quarter of 2023.

evidence.text: The second quarter of 2023 exhibited elevated transportation rates, moderating consumer demand, and capacity entering the market.

The declines in pricing and purchased transportation costs were driven by the excess carrier capacity and continued weak consumer demand discussed in the market trends and business trends sections above. The global forwarding market is facing continued weak demand and excess carrier capacity.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Input costs are experiencing a decline, particularly evident in transportation services. This reduction is supported by market conditions such as weak demand and excess carrier capacity, which have contributed to lower purchased transportation costs and higher adjusted gross profits.

evidence.text: Our average truckload linehaul cost per mile, excluding fuel surcharges, decreased approximately 19.0 percent during the second quarter of 2023.

Purchased transportation costs and pricing have remained suppressed in 2023 due to the continued weak demand and excess carrier capacity in the market. The cost of purchased transportation decreased relative to our contractual rates resulting in elevated adjusted gross profits per transaction in the six months ended June 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with decreases attributed to cost optimization efforts, lower variable compensation, and reduced average employee headcount.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased 15.2 percent to $377.3 million, primarily due to cost optimization efforts and lower variable compensation.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to cost optimization efforts including lower average employee headcount in addition to lower variable compensation reflecting the decline in results relative to the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, driven by cost optimization efforts and lower personnel expenses.

evidence.text: The average routing guide depth of tender in the second quarter of 2023 declined to 1.1, which is the lowest level we have seen since the pandemic impacted the second quarter of 2020.

Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to cost optimization efforts including lower average employee headcount in addition to lower variable compensation reflecting the decline in results relative to the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Margins are compressing due to lower pricing and volumes across multiple service lines, driven by excess capacity and weak demand in the transportation and global forwarding markets.

evidence.text: Adjusted operating margin of 19.9 percent declined 2,560 basis points.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Our transportation adjusted gross profits decreased due to lower adjusted gross profit per transaction in truckload and ocean services, and to a lesser extent air and LTL services, in addition to decreased volumes in nearly all service lines. The lower adjusted gross profit per transaction was driven by the excess capacity and weak demand in the surface transportation and global forwarding markets discussed in the market trends and business trends sections above, which have suppressed freight rates in the six months ended June 30, 2023.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: The supply chain health is showing signs of improvement, though it continues to face some challenges.

evidence.text: Several consecutive quarters of weak consumer demand have nearly eliminated the challenges from port congestion and transportation equipment shortages that were impacting the global forwarding market in recent years.

The prior year period benefited from the softening market conditions as the cost of purchased transportation decreased relative to our contractual rates resulting in elevated adjusted gross profits per transaction in the six months ended June 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing due to excess carrier capacity and weak consumer demand.

evidence.text: Carrier capacity in the North America surface transportation market has remained plentiful relative to demand which has resulted in suppressed transportation rates for much of the second quarter of 2023.

The declines in pricing and purchased transportation costs were driven by the excess carrier capacity and continued weak consumer demand discussed in the market trends and business trends sections above.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Six Months Ended June 30, 2023 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2022
